paragraphs.body: Growing up in Germany you are already born into soccer already. As a boy, you just at least have to try playing soccer and if you aren't any good at it, you at least have to try it. My Dad was a soccer player and my Grandfather was his coach for some time in his career and always coached soccer teams his whole life and even played before that. So, before I was even born it was already sure I was going to be a soccer player. And to be honest, to this day I haven’t thought about quitting once. Soccer has brought me so much joy and friends. Almost all of my friends to this day have come from teams I played on. It’s just such a sense of community in a team that I value a lot. Especially after getting to an academy you slowly start to realize that your teammates are becoming your family. If you practice 5 days a week and play a game on the weekends these are the people that you see the most, sometimes even more than your family. These circumstances create a community that is difficult to understand for a person who has never been part of a team. You might not always like everyone on the whole roster, but you surely create a bond with everyone and some will be your best friends. Back in Germany I also always loved going to the stadium with my friends and my dad. Seeing the game was of course the main focus, but also seeing the fans and their tifos was very impressive. That’s a thing that is really different between the U.S. and Germany. The fan scene is very different, I feel like in the U.S. people go to games to have an entertaining afternoon, but in Germany, especially the so-called "Ultras" the hooligans, would die for their club, and for them, it is just about if their team wins or looses. That is another way how soccer creates a community for people, these fans travel all across the land to get to their team's games and also have the same purpose of supporting their team.

annotations.text: That is another way how soccer creates a community for people, these fans travel all across the land to get to their team's games and also have the same purpose of supporting their team.
annotations.comment: You intro some fascinating, and intriguing, info about the Ultras but then don't expand on it. Are they important to your essay? If so, develop :)

e. That’s a thing that is really

paragraphs.body: Community is a very important thing for me because I think that is what makes you feel at home and what makes you feel comfortable. If you think about it almost everything isn’t as fun when your friends or family are not doing it with you. That is why I always cherish time with those people. My communities have always been my parents that just have always been there for me and supported me. Another community that makes life so much more fun is my friends, these are the people I can have fun with but also always support me throughout my journey. And then there would be my girlfriend, you could say that’s not really a community as she is only one person, but she's also someone who always supports me and gives me comfort. We met in kindergarten and did not get together until we were 16 years old, but ever since then we basically share everything with each other, she knows everything that is going on in my life and I know everything that is going on in her life. All these communities have such a high value for me and sort of give me a place in the “universe”. Unfortunately, this summer I had to leave all that behind. I made the decision to change my life completely and go to college in the U.S., which ultimately meant: leaving all those communities and people I just wrote about, behind me and going to a new country 12 hours on a plane away and with a time difference of 6 hours from home. So, I literally changed my place in the world. But after the first two weeks when I FaceTimed my parents one day, I said:" I thought this was going to be harder." I didn't even miss them that much. Before coming here, I was really scared that I was going to be so homesick and miss my parents, my girlfriend, and my friends so much, that I couldn't even enjoy the experience. But what I forgot about was that I was going to get put in a completely new community, where most of the people had already been through what I was going through at that time. My team made me feel comfortable right away and everything was fun, so I couldn’t even really think about being home and start to feel homesick.

annotations.context_left: which ultimately meant: leaving all those communities and people I just wrote about, behind me and

paragraphs.body: That experience made me realize I don't really have a physical place in the world, my place in the world is where I am surrounded by people that make me feel comfortable. And in my case, that's two places, back home in Germany and my new home here in Pittsburgh.

annotations.text: That experience made me realize I don't really have a physical place in the world, my place in the world is where I am surrounded by people that make me feel comfortable.

paragraphs.body: I think that can be a lesson for anyone reading this because I think many people are scared to make a step like that because they don't want to go too far away from home and rather stay in their comfort zone. I encourage everyone to step out of their comfort zone because that is something that will shape your personality and ultimately make you a greater person. You will probably face adversity and I am not saying that you are going to feel at home right away but if you trust the process it is going to be a great experience. A lot of my friends go to college 35 Minutes by car from our hometown. That is just something I never really wanted to do, but I was still scared of leaving my home and- what I thought was- my place in the world. But now after living in the U.S. for 5 months now, I can say that you can create a home for yourself everywhere in the world as long as you have the right people around you that make you feel like you are home.

annotations.text: I encourage everyone to step out of their comfort zone because that is something that will shape your personality and ultimately make you a greater person. You will probably face adversity and I am not saying that you are going to feel at home right away but if you trust the process it is going to be a great experience.
